Smoking. For those who smell cigarrete, do not get close to our mosque. Smoking clearly can harm himself and even can disturb other people.  Passive smoker could feel its impact.  The wives and children can be impacted. And so is bad effects as a result of cigarette smell that can cause disruption to concentration of congregation during mass prayer.  We feel uncomfortable if in our right side standing a smoker with disturbing bad breath. Could such person attending mass prayer with bad breath? The problem of law in smoking. Cigarette comes from infidel country. Muslim knew it in the 10th Hijriyah (Islamic Calendar).  British slipped these cigarettes among Muslims during Daulah Utsmaniyah time. The beginning was a Jew came to the Maghribi country briging ciggarets and he claimed them as medicine.  The cigarettes eventually spread to Egypt and other Muslim countries.  In the beginning of cigarrete invention, the ulama argued on its law.  Some said it was haram, some said makruh, some said mubah. However, the law of somking is haram. Some reasons that strengthen this opinion: 1) we should not harm ourself and do so towards other people. Allah said “and do not destroy one another: for, behold, God is indeed a dispenser of grace unto you (Chapter An Nisaa’:29). In other verse, Allah said “and let not your own hands throw you into destruction (Chapter Al-Baqoroh: 195). A hadith also mentioned not to harm other people, as said by Prophet Muhammad “You should not give bad impact (mudhorot) to other people and revenge against them ” (Hadith narrated by HR. Ibnu Majah no. 2340, Ad Daruquthni 3/77, Al Baihaqi 6/69, Al Hakim 2/66. Syaikh Al Albani said this hadits is authentic). The hadith clearly forbid causing mudhorot to other people and is inclusive of smoking. Tven the doctors has studied that cigarettes carry various types of poisons. The research also showed that 25 million people died a year mainly because of smoking. This could be an evidence that smoking can harm oneself; 2)- Human body does not belong that person; 3) Smoking is something that is khobits (dirty). Nabi shallallahu ‘alaihi wa sallam’s personality is that he allowed the thoyyib (good) ones and forbade the khobits (bad) ones. It is clear that the smell of cigarretes is not liked; Attending Mass Prayer with the breath of cigarettes.  In this matter, the ulama argued on whether someone with cigarette smell can participate in mass prayer.  Some said it was haram, other said makruh.  Precise opininon in this matter was that participationg in mass prayer for such people are forbidden.  Some of the reasons: 1- Hadits Ibnu ‘Umar radhiyallahu ‘anhuma, Prophet Muhammad shallallahu ‘alaihi wa sallam said, “Whoever eats this plant –which is onion-, hence do not get closer to our mosque” (Hadith Narrated by Bukhari no. 853 and Muslim no. 561). Prophet shallallahu ‘alaihi wa sallam forbade this because those with bad breath will harm other jamaah (congregation). Hence, the same thing was caused by cigarretes.2- Allah Ta’ala said, “And as for those who malign believing men and believing women without their having done any wrong - they surely burden themselves with the guilt of calumny, and [thus] with a flagrant sin!” (Chapter Al Ahzab: 58). The smell of cigarretes indeed harmed the mukmin (believing people); 3- A hadits qudsi mentioned, “Whoever harms my guardian (believing people/the mukmin), hence I allow you to fight against them” (Hadith narrated by Bukhari no. 6502). Herewith the reasons of attending mass prayers for those who had cigarrette breath are not allowed.
